meta.name: Historical Calibration Reuse Automation
meta.purpose: Create a new calibration certificate draft by matching a new user-entered instrument job against previously extracted historical certificate data.
meta.primary_guardrail: Historical values may be reused only as a tagged draft requiring engineer review and approval. Generated certificates must preserve provenance and must not imply fresh measurement unless the laboratory SOP permits that workflow.

sample_pdf_observations.source_file: AA Electro Magnetic Test Laboratory Private Limited-2.pdf
important_edge_cases: One uploaded PDF can contain many separate certificates.
Most certificates are one page, but some are multi-page certificates sharing the same Certificate/ULR number.
Continuation pages may contain result rows without repeating the instrument identification block.
Calibration result table schemas vary by discipline and instrument type.

historical_documents.purpose: Tracks each uploaded source document before and after extraction.

certificates.purpose: One logical certificate record, historical or generated.

calibration_result_sections.purpose: Represents each result table or result group, for example AC Voltage, Resistance, Force, Thermal Uniformity.

calibration_result_rows.purpose: Stores reusable measured values, uncertainty values, correction/error values, and calibration points.

master_equipment.purpose: Stores reference equipment used to produce the historical calibration result.

calibration_jobs.purpose: Tracks new user-entered jobs and their matching/generation lifecycle.

field_provenance.purpose: Audits every copied, user-entered, auto-generated, and engineer-edited field.

workflow.purpose: Accept historical certificate PDFs, images, Excel files, or observation sheets and register them for extraction.
validation: File must be readable.
Checksum must not already exist unless reprocessing is requested.
PDF page count must be greater than zero.
error_handling: Reject unreadable files with a user-visible message.
Mark duplicate uploads as skipped and link to the existing extraction.

workflow.purpose: Split uploaded PDFs into logical certificate records using Certificate/ULR number, page counters, and repeated certificate headers.
dependencies: page image renderer for scanned PDFs
validation: Every page must be assigned to exactly one certificate group.
A repeated ULR with Page 1 of N through Page N of N must be grouped as one certificate.
A missing ULR must be marked for AI or human review.
error_handling: If page grouping is ambiguous, group conservatively and mark extraction_quality=needs_review.
If scanned pages have no text, route to Gemini multimodal extraction.

workflow.purpose: Extract header metadata, UUC identification, environmental conditions, master equipment, result sections, result rows, notes, and signatures from each certificate group.
validation: certificate_ulr_no is required.
instrument_name is required on the first page of a certificate.
discipline_parameter is required.
At least one calibration result row is required for reusable records.
All result sections must preserve raw headers and normalized rows.
error_handling: If rule extraction and AI extraction disagree, preserve both values and mark the field conflict.
If result rows are missing, store the header but mark quality_status=incomplete.

retry_logic.fallback: Run Gemini with page images plus extracted text

workflow.purpose: Clean extracted values, normalize units, standardize field names, calculate quality scores, and detect suspicious data.
validation: Dates must parse to ISO dates.
Coverage factor k should be captured when stated.
Uncertainty values must be numeric when possible.
Master equipment valid_upto should not be earlier than calibration_date without a warning.
Result rows must keep both raw and normalized values.
error_handling: Ambiguous units remain as raw text and are marked needs_unit_review.
Low-confidence fields are stored but excluded from automatic reuse until reviewed.

workflow.purpose: Store validated historical certificates, result sections, result rows, master equipment, and provenance in PostgreSQL.
validation: certificate_ulr_no must be unique per historical source unless it is a multi-page continuation.
All result rows must reference a result section.
Database write must be atomic.
error_handling: Rollback on partial write failure.
If duplicate ULR appears with different source content, create a conflict record for review.

workflow.purpose: Collect new client and instrument input from the CalCert UI.
validation: client_name, instrument_name, discipline_parameter, and range_text are required.
discipline_parameter must match a configured discipline.
serial_number is strongly recommended and should be flagged if missing.
error_handling: Show inline validation errors in the UI.
Save incomplete job as draft only if the user chooses to save.

workflow.purpose: Find historical certificates that could match the new job using hard filters, fuzzy text search, numeric/unit matching, and calibration-point overlap.
validation: Candidate confidence scores must be between 0 and 1.
Candidates with quality_status=bad must be excluded.
Candidates with incomplete result rows must be eligible only for partial reuse.
error_handling: If pg_trgm is unavailable, fall back to ILIKE plus rapidfuzz scoring in Python.
If no candidates score above threshold, route to no-match workflow.

workflow.purpose: Classify ranked candidates into auto-prefill, engineer-selection, or no-match paths.
validation: High-confidence candidates still require engineer approval before certificate generation.
Tied candidates must never be auto-selected.
Selected candidate must have at least one reusable result section.
error_handling: If top candidates are tied, present side-by-side comparison.
If all candidates are incomplete, route to partial-reuse or fresh-calibration workflow.

workflow.purpose: Create a new draft certificate by combining new user-provided fields with reusable historical fields from the selected candidate.
validation: New client fields must replace historical client fields.
New certificate number, job number, calibration date, due date, serial number, and customer details must be generated or taken from the new job.
Copied historical result values must be tagged source_type=historical.
Every field must have provenance.
error_handling: Missing reusable field is marked requires_fresh_measurement.
Invalid date calculation defaults to configured 12-month validity and logs a warning.

workflow.purpose: Allow an engineer to review, compare, edit, approve, reject, or send the job to fresh calibration.
validation: Engineer must explicitly confirm the reuse decision.
Any edited value must store edited_by, edited_at, and previous_value.
Approval is blocked if required fields are empty.
error_handling: Reject sends the job to relaxed search, manual selection, or fresh calibration.
Session timeout auto-saves the draft.

workflow.purpose: Generate the final PDF certificate from the approved draft using the configured certificate template.
validation: PDF must contain certificate number, client name, instrument name, calibration date, result rows, and audit footer.
Generated PDF size must exceed a minimum configured threshold.
PDF text extraction must find the new certificate number.
All reused values must be present in the field provenance table.
error_handling: If the selected template fails, retry with a default template.
If PDF generation fails after retries, retain approved JSON payload and notify the engineer.

workflow.purpose: Record engineer approvals, rejections, edits, and manual selections to improve future matching.
validation: Feedback must reference the job and candidate certificate.
Rejected matches must store the rejection reason.
error_handling: If feedback write fails, queue the event for background retry.

candidate_retrieval: Hard filter by discipline_parameter where available.
Use pg_trgm similarity on instrument_name, manufacturer, model, range_text, and least_count_text.
Retrieve the top 50 raw candidates for scoring.
Optionally include semantic vector search for synonyms such as voltmeter and voltage meter.
ranking_rules: If scores are within tie_delta, require engineer selection.
Prefer newer calibration dates only as a tiebreaker.
Exclude records with expired master equipment unless engineer explicitly allows historical reuse with a warning.
Prefer candidates whose result sections cover all requested calibration points.

reuse_from_historical_dataset: instrument_name if user did not override
certificate_generation_rules.mandatory_audit_footer: Values marked as historical were retrieved from source certificate {source_certificate_ulr_no}, reviewed by {engineer_name}, and approved on {approval_date}.

no_match_found.response: Offer fresh calibration, relaxed search, or manual historical selection.

multiple_high_confidence_matches.trigger: Two or more candidates above 0.85 and within 0.05 score delta.
multiple_high_confidence_matches.response: Show side-by-side candidate comparison and require engineer selection.

historical_dataset_incomplete.trigger: Selected candidate is missing required result sections or rows.
historical_dataset_incomplete.response: Prefill available fields and mark missing fields requires_fresh_measurement.

database_unavailable.trigger: PostgreSQL or Supabase cannot be reached.
database_unavailable.response: Save job, queue matching, retry in background, and show queued status.

pdf_generation_failed.trigger: PDF rendering fails or validation cannot find required text.
pdf_generation_failed.response: Retry with default template, then preserve approved JSON for manual generation.

compliance_block.trigger: Workflow attempts to generate a certificate without review or provenance.
compliance_block.response: Block generation and require engineer approval.
